title: Continuous-Time: Tracking a Sinusoid

runtime.expected_outcome: After a warm-up, the filter's x estimate tracks sin(t) within a small envelope; plotting x vs y shows clear smoothing.

math.latex: x \leftarrow x + dt \, (v - \kappa \pi_s (x - y)),\quad v \leftarrow v + dt \, (-\kappa \pi_f v)

audiences.kid: Imagine tracking a bouncing ball. Your eyes follow it; the ball moves smoothly; your guess is always close.
audiences.real: Generalised-coordinate Bayes filter: the state carries position AND velocity. Smoothing emerges from the dynamics prior.
audiences.equation: x, v updates are gradient steps on Gaussian free energy with precision weights on sensor and dynamics errors.
audiences.derivation: Eq 8.1 regime: state in generalised coords (x, x'). Free energy F = 0.5 (pi_s (x-y)^2 + pi_f v^2). Gradient flow recovers a Kalman-like filter (Friston 2008).

orchestrate.objective: Run the generalized filter against a 1 Hz sinusoid and verify tracking.
orchestrate.context: sinusoid_tracker fixture; 400 steps at dt=0.05s; pi_s=20, pi_f=1.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 8.
Friston (2008), Hierarchical models in the brain.
